title: Efficacy and Safety of Non-invasive Brain Stimulation for Mild Cognitive Impairment and Early-Stage Alzheimer's Disease: A Systematic Review and Meta-analysis of Randomized Controlled Trials
objectives: To systematically review and synthesize evidence from randomized controlled trials on the efficacy and safety of non-invasive brain stimulation (rTMS, tDCS, tACS) for improving cognitive, functional, and neuropsychiatric outcomes in adults with Mild Cognitive Impairment or early-stage Alzheimer's Disease.
eligibility: We will include randomized controlled trials (RCTs) and crossover trials comparing non-invasive brain stimulation (NIBS) against sham, no intervention, or active control in adults (≥18 years) with a diagnosis of MCI or mild AD. Studies must report on at least one outcome of interest, including global cognition, safety, activities of daily living, or neuropsychiatric symptoms.
information_sources: A comprehensive search will be conducted in PubMed, Embase, Cochrane Central Register of Controlled Trials (CENTRAL), PsycINFO, and Web of Science from January 2000 onwards. Trial registries (e.g., ClinicalTrials.gov) and reference lists of included studies will also be screened.
search_strategy_description: The search strategy will combine subject headings (e.g., MeSH, Emtree) and free-text terms for two main concepts: (1) the population (MCI, Alzheimer's Disease) and (2) the intervention (rTMS, tDCS, tACS). The search will be supplemented with a validated filter to identify randomized controlled trials.
study_selection_process: Two reviewers will independently screen titles and abstracts, followed by full-text articles, against the predefined eligibility criteria. Disagreements will be resolved through discussion or consultation with a third reviewer. A PRISMA flow diagram will document the selection process.
data_extraction_plan: Two reviewers will independently extract data using a standardized form. Extracted data will include study characteristics, participant demographics, intervention and comparator details (modality, parameters, duration), outcome measures, and results. Discrepancies will be resolved by consensus.
risk_of_bias_tool: Cochrane Risk of Bias tool for randomized trials (RoB 2)
statistical_methods: Meta-analyses will be performed using a random-effects model. For continuous outcomes, Standardized Mean Differences (SMD) or Mean Differences (MD) will be calculated. For dichotomous outcomes (e.g., adverse events), Risk Ratios (RR) will be calculated. Statistical heterogeneity will be assessed using the I² statistic. Publication bias will be explored using funnel plots and Egger's test if sufficient studies are available.
